businessSummary.oneLiner: The undisputed leader in AI computing, dominating global AI infrastructure with data center GPUs and accelerators
businessSummary.description: NVIDIA leads GPU-based AI accelerated computing with a vertically integrated ecosystem from data center AI chips to software platforms. At GTC 2026, the company unveiled the Vera Rubin and Vera CPU platforms as part of a 5-layer full-stack AI strategy. FY26 revenue reached $215.9B (+65% YoY) with $120B in net income. The company projects $1 trillion in cumulative Blackwell and Rubin chip orders through 2027.
howTheyMakeMoney: Data center AI accelerators (80%+ of revenue)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Compressed from 45x to 36.63x due to DeepSeek shock and macro headwinds. Contrarians see this as increased valuation attractiveness

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E (FY27E)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Lower than Alphabet and AMD. The most reasonable valuation among AI growth stocks given FY26 revenue of $215.9B (+65%)

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (FY26)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Unprecedented growth at this scale in semiconductor history. Data center AI demand is the core driver

keyMetrics.name: Net Income
keyMetrics.interpretation: Among the highest annual net incomes for any US company. Dominant profitability

keyMetrics.name: FCF (Free Cash Flow)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Record shareholder returns announced at GTC 2026, including buybacks and dividend increases

keyMetrics.name: Cumulative Order Forecast
keyMetrics.interpretation: Blackwell + Rubin chip orders doubled from prior $500B forecast

growthDrivers.title: Risk-on rally lifts NVDA to $179.45 (+2.43%)
growthDrivers.description: Broad tech rally on March 25 as geopolitical tensions eased. NVDA bounced from $175.20, defending the low end of its 6-week $170-$198 range

growthDrivers.title: $1 trillion cumulative AI chip orders through 2027
growthDrivers.description: CEO Jensen Huang announced at GTC 2026 that Blackwell and Vera Rubin orders are projected to reach at least $1T, doubling the prior $500B forecast

growthDrivers.title: Rosenblatt raises target to $325 (3/18)
growthDrivers.description: Analyst Kevin Cassidy updated his model after management meetings, raising NVDA target from $300 to $325

growthDrivers.title: New Street Research adds NVDA to '2026 Best Ideas' (3/19)
growthDrivers.description: Pierre Ferragu named NVDA his top pick, citing long-term revenue potential exceeding market expectations

growthDrivers.title: Record shareholder returns planned
growthDrivers.description: GTC 2026 announced massive buyback and dividend expansion. FCF of $60B+ provides ample capacity

growthDrivers.title: Energy partnerships for AI factory model
growthDrivers.description: Partnerships with AES, Constellation Energy, and NextEra Energy to operate AI data centers as flexible grid assets

competitiveAdvantage.summary: CUDA ecosystem lock-in, GPU+CPU+networking+software vertical integration, annual architecture roadmap, overwhelming economies of scale

moats.description: 5M+ CUDA developers. Switching costs are prohibitively high, maintaining GPU market dominance
Full-stack: GPU, CPU (Vera), networking (Spectrum-X), and software (CUDA, NIM, NemoClaw)
Hopper to Blackwell to Vera Rubin annual cadence maintains 2-3 year tech lead over competitors
FY26 revenue of $215.9B is the largest in semiconductors. R&D spending dwarfs competitors

competitors.name: AMD
competitors.detail: MI300X entered AI GPU market but trails significantly in software ecosystem and scale. Under 10% DC AI share

competitors.name: Google TPU
competitors.detail: Internal AI chips strong for Google's own use. Limited external market influence

competitors.name: Amazon Trainium
competitors.detail: AWS-optimized chip. Falls behind NVIDIA in general-purpose capabilities

competitors.name: Broadcom
competitors.detail: Custom ASICs for hyperscalers. Cannot compete with NVIDIA in the general market

recentNews.headline: Risk-on rally sends NVDA to $179.45 (+2.43%), defends $170 support
recentNews.significance: Broad tech recovery on easing geopolitical tensions. Held the bottom of 6-week trading range

recentNews.headline: Wilsey Asset Management sells NVDA shares
recentNews.significance: Institutional position reduction continues alongside insider selling, maintaining short-term sell pressure

recentNews.headline: Director Mark Stevens sells 221,682 shares
recentNews.significance: Board member sold over 220K shares in one day. Insider selling pattern continues to weigh on sentiment

recentNews.headline: Jensen Huang declares 'AGI achieved' on podcast
recentNews.significance: CEO claimed AGI milestone but tempered near-term expectations for AI agent commercialization

recentNews.headline: New Street Research adds NVDA to '2026 Best Ideas'
recentNews.significance: Pierre Ferragu cites long-term revenue potential exceeding market consensus

analystOpinions.notableComment: Rosenblatt $325, New Street 'Best Ideas.' Overwhelming Buy consensus. P/E compression from 45x to 36.6x has increased valuation appeal. Next earnings May 20

risks.title: Persistent insider + institutional selling
risks.description: Director Stevens sold 220K+ shares followed by Wilsey AM selling. Simultaneous insider and institutional reduction is a caution signal

risks.title: Macro headwinds: Oil $99+, Nasdaq -15%
risks.description: Iran crisis pushing oil above $99, Fed rates at 3.5-3.75%, Nasdaq down 15% from February peak

risks.title: DeepSeek shock: AI possible with fewer GPUs?
risks.description: January's DeepSeek demonstrated competitive AI models built with far fewer GPUs. Key driver of P/E compression from 45x to 36.6x

risks.title: Export control tightening (Supermicro smuggling case)
risks.description: Supermicro co-founder indicted for smuggling $2.5B worth of NVIDIA chips to China. May trigger stricter controls

risks.title: AI agent commercialization delay
risks.description: Huang himself tempered near-term expectations. AGI achieved but commercial products need more time

buyReasons.title: P/E compression to 36.6x, lowest among AI growth stocks
buyReasons.rationale: Down from 45x. Forward P/E of 22x is below Alphabet and AMD. Same earnings at a cheaper price

buyReasons.title: $1T order pipeline + analyst upgrade rush
buyReasons.rationale: Rosenblatt $325, New Street 'Best Ideas.' Post-GTC target upgrades continuing

buyReasons.title: $170 range support held in 6-week trading range
buyReasons.rationale: Successfully defended the bottom of $170-$198 range on risk-on rally

buyReasons.title: Record shareholder returns + dividend increase expected
buyReasons.rationale: FCF of $60B+ funding massive buybacks and dividend expansion

overallOpinion: The risk-on rally pushed NVDA to $179.45 (+2.43%), successfully defending the $170 floor of its 6-week trading range. However, insider selling (director 220K shares) and institutional exits (Wilsey AM) continue to create short-term pressure.

P/E has compressed from 45x to 36.6x due to the DeepSeek shock and oil above $99. But flip the narrative: a Forward P/E of 22x with a $1T order pipeline means the world's most profitable tech company is trading at a discount. Rosenblatt's $325 target and New Street's 'Best Ideas' selection support this view.
Below both the 50-day ($184) and 200-day ($184) with a death cross intact. But the $170 support hold and RSI at 44 suggest a bottoming process. Consensus target $276 (+54%). Next earnings May 20.

investmentVerdict.summary: At P/E 36.6x (Forward 22x) with a $1T order pipeline, the death cross and insider selling represent a buying opportunity created by macro compression, not fundamental deterioration. Dollar-cost averaging is appropriate given oil and rate uncertainty.
investmentVerdict.horizon: 6-12 months (next earnings May 20 + Vera Rubin shipments)

threads.hook: Multiple compression is creating a buying opportunity
threads.text:
$NVDA (Mar 26) $179.45

Risk-on rally delivered a 2.43% bounce, defending the $170 floor of the 6-week range. P/E compressed from 45x to 36.6x. Forward P/E of 22x is the cheapest in AI.

Insiders and institutions are selling, but Rosenblatt raised to $325 and New Street named it 'Best Idea of 2026.' $1T order pipeline, $120B net income. Fundamentally the strongest it's ever been.

Oil $99, Nasdaq -15%. When macro turns, this is the first tech stock to recover. Consensus $276 (+54%).

witty.hook: AGI declared, then oil at $99 beat AI at its own game
witty.text:
$NVDA (Mar 26) $179.45

AGI achieved, or so Jensen says. Meanwhile P/E went from 45 to 36 because oil at $99 and a -15% Nasdaq don't care about artificial general intelligence.

Forward P/E 22x with $1T in orders. World's most profitable company on clearance. Directors sold 220K shares and waved goodbye.

Consensus $276 (+54%). When macro resolves, this stock leads the recovery. Timing is everything.

empathy.hook: Death cross and insider selling, but the company is historically strong
empathy.text:
$NVDA (Mar 26) $179.45

Death cross with insiders and institutions selling. Seeing those headlines makes anyone nervous. Today's 2.43% bounce is welcome but the road ahead looks bumpy.

Forward P/E 22x, $1T orders, $120B net income. The company has never been in better shape. The pullback is about oil and macro, not NVIDIA itself.

Consensus $276 (+54%). Next earnings May 20. Patience will be rewarded.
